comments.author: GridGazer
comments.text: A four-time world champion, a current F1 race winner, and a rising star recovering from injury — sharing an Audi station wagon at the Nürburgring. This is the most beautifully absurd entry in endurance racing history. Rossi's piece captures the layers perfectly: Keisner celebrating his breakthrough Suzuka win, Koopman targeting the Nordschleife as his comeback from a back injury, and Vettel returning to competitive driving for the first time since 2022. The RS6 Avant GT3 vs BMW M3 Touring super-wagon duel is going to break the internet. The SPX class can't win overall, but it'll win the headlines. The race is now set for 16–17 May — just under seven weeks away — and the fact that Koopman is confident enough in his recovery to commit this early is genuinely encouraging after that scary Suzuka crash.

replies.author: WilliamsFaithful
replies.text: KOOPMAN IS COMING BACK. Not just to F1, but to the Nordschleife. After that lap-one crash in Japan, seeing him stand by the car was terrifying. Now he's targeting a 24-hour endurance race in mid-May as his comeback. The mental strength that takes is immense. TEF is doing an incredible job filling in — a podium in Bahrain! — but knowing Lucas is on the mend and planning a return makes this even better. Williams' future is bright.

replies.author: PaddockInsider
replies.text: The timing is interesting. Koopman's recovery timeline now has a clear target — mid-May. TEF's three-race stint runs through Bahrain, Saudi Arabia, and Miami. If Koopman is fit enough for the Nürburgring 24H in May, he's fit enough to return to the Williams seat by Canada in June. The Nordschleife entry is essentially a public fitness test — 'I'm back, and I'm ready.' Smart PR, but also genuinely ballsy.

replies.author: VettelReturns
replies.text: SEBASTIAN VETTEL IS RACING AGAIN. I don't care that it's a non-championship event. I don't care that it's an experimental station wagon. The man won 53 grands prix, four world championships, and he's been away since 2022. His passion for sustainability and innovative projects makes the RS6 Avant GT3 concept a perfect fit. Keisner and Koopman get to share a car with a legend. The Nordschleife on 16–17 May just became a pilgrimage site for every F1 fan alive.

replies.author: ChaosReigns
replies.text: Vettel, Keisner, and Koopman walk into an Audi station wagon at the Nürburgring. This isn't a joke setup — it's the actual 2026 N24 entry. The combined career stats: 4 world championships, 53 F1 wins, 1 current F1 win, and 1 back injury recovery. The RS6 Avant GT3 vs BMW M3 Touring battle is going to be the most watched SPX class duel in history. The 'Ring has seen everything, but a super-wagon shootout with a four-time champion in mid-May? This is new.

comments.author: AudiRising
comments.text: Audi are having quite the season. Keisner wins in Japan, becomes P2 in the F1 championship, and now he's headlining a Nürburgring 24H entry alongside Vettel and Koopman in an RS6 Avant GT3. The marketing department in Ingolstadt must be working overtime. The RS6 Avant GT3 is an experimental SPX class car — a high-performance estate that's never been raced before — and they're putting it against a BMW M3 Touring. This is the kind of brand warfare that makes endurance racing special. And the driver lineup is a cross-generational masterpiece: Vettel (the legend), Keisner (the present), Koopman (the future). All three with Audi connections. The 16–17 May weekend is circled on my calendar.

replies.author: FourRingsOrBust
replies.text: The RS6 Avant GT3 is the wildcard. It's an estate car. A station wagon. Racing at the Nordschleife. The SPX class doesn't require homologation, so Audi can push the concept as far as they want. If it's quick, the memes will be legendary. If it's not, the memes will still be legendary. But with Vettel and Keisner behind the wheel, I'm betting on quick.

replies.author: SbinnalaEnjoyer
replies.text: An Audi estate car vs a BMW estate car, driven by a four-time champion, a current F1 winner, and a recovering rookie. The Nürburgring 24H has officially peaked. Everything after this is a downgrade.

comments.author: ChaosReigns
comments.text: The Nürburgring 24H entries this year are already stacking up to be incredible. MaraDöner, Väisänen, and Thompson are rumoured for a Porsche in SP9 Pro. Now Keisner, Koopman, and Vettel confirmed in an Audi RS6 Avant GT3 in SPX. Plus the BMW M3 Touring rival. The F1 paddock is using the mid-May break to invade the Nordschleife, and I am completely here for it. The SPX class is going to have more star power than some F1 grids. 16–17 May can't come soon enough.

replies.author: VoiceOfTheFan
replies.text: A STATION WAGON WITH VETTEL AND KEISNER AT THE NORDSCHLEIFE. I DON'T KNOW WHAT SPX MEANS BUT I NEED TO WATCH THIS. ALSO KOOPMAN IS BACK. THIS IS THE COOLEST THING EVER.

replies.author: MaraDönerFan42
replies.text: The MaraDöner-Porsche rumours are still unconfirmed, but if that entry happens alongside this Audi super-team, the N24 becomes an unofficial F1 reunion. The paddock is going to be empty in mid-May because everyone's in Germany driving GT cars.

comments.author: SpringfieldStan
comments.text: No Springfield in this entry, but she's already shown she can handle endurance racing — remember her Daytona 500 debut where she beat Wright? The Nürburgring would suit her wet-weather skills. Maybe Aston Martin will enter something by May. A girl can dream.

comments.author: WrightBackWhereIBelong
comments.text: Wright did the Daytona 500 and finished P7. Keisner is doing the Nürburgring 24H in a station wagon. The crossover era is real. These drivers just want to race anything, anywhere. Respect. Maybe Wright will find an entry for the N24 too — his endurance background makes him a natural fit.

replies.author: NASCARtoF1
replies.text: Wright in a stock car at Daytona, Keisner in an Audi wagon at the Nordschleife, Springfield in a stock car beating Wright. The 2026 season is just a collection of drivers doing side quests. And every one of them is delivering. By mid-May, the N24 grid could be half F1 drivers.

comments.author: Zwart_Horse_187
comments.text: Keisner gets to share a car with Vettel at the Nürburgring. Zwart gets to share a garage with a shopping trolley. The contrast is painful.

replies.author: SilverArrowsFan
replies.text: Mercedes should enter an AMG GT3 for Zwart at the N24. Let him blow off some steam on the Nordschleife. Maybe he'd come back happier. Or maybe he'd call it a shopping trolley too. Either way, entertainment.

comments.author: FaktLap1Legend
comments.text: Vettel, Keisner, Koopman — all sharing an Audi wagon in May. Cute. The GOAT Sam Fakt doesn't need teammates, doesn't need an estate car, doesn't need the Nordschleife. He's already completed the Nürburgring 24 Hours in his mind, solo, in an F0 chassis that hasn't been built yet. The podium celebrations are merely a formality. 🐐

replies.author: DNF_Fakt
replies.text: Vettel is a four-time world champion. Keisner just won a grand prix. Koopman is recovering from a back injury and showing incredible courage. And you're still on about F0. Let the real drivers have their moment. Please.

comments.author: StatsNerd_26
comments.text: Quick numbers on the driver lineup: Vettel — 4 world championships, 53 wins, 57 poles, 122 podiums, 299 entries. Keisner — 1 win, 41 points, P2 in the 2026 F1CC standings. Koopman — 3 points in 2026 before injury, P6 qualifying in Melbourne, fastest lap in China. Combined: 54 grand prix wins, 4 titles, and one very famous station wagon. The RS6 Avant GT3 has more championship pedigree than some F1 teams.

replies.author: ConstructorsMathematician
replies.text: The combined marketability of this entry is off the charts. Audi are leveraging their F1 programme, their heritage (Vettel's legacy transcends engine suppliers), and their future (Keisner). The RS6 Avant GT3 will be the most photographed car at the N24 in May, regardless of where it finishes.
